volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hippolytus (Section 1435 - Section 1465)

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: καὶ σοὶ παραινῶ πατέρα μὴ στυγεῖν σέθεν ,
sections.translation: And thee Hippolytus , I admonish ; hate not thy sire , for in this death thou dost but meet thy destined fate .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: καὶ χαῖρ ’· ἐμοὶ γὰρ οὐ θέμις φθιτοὺς ὁρᾶν
sections.translation: And now farewell ! ’ tis not for me to gaze upon the dead , or pollute my sight with death-scenes , and e ’ en now I see thee nigh that evil moment . [*] [ Exit Artemis .

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: χαίρουσα καὶ σὺ στεῖχε , παρθέν ’ ὀλβία ·
sections.translation: Farewell , blest virgin queen ! leave me now ! How easily [*] thou resignest our long friendship ! I am reconciled with my father at thy desire , yea , for ever before I would obey thy bidding .

sections.label: Section 1444
sections.original: αἰαῖ , κατ ’ ὄσσων κιγχάνει μ ’ ἤδη σκότος ·
sections.translation: Ah me ! the darkness is settling even now upon my eyes .

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: λαβοῦ , πάτερ , μου καὶ κατόρθωσον δέμας .
sections.translation: Take me , father , in thy arms , lift me up .

sections.label: Section 1446
sections.original: οἴμοι , τέκνον , τί δρᾷς με τὸν δυσδαίμονα ;
sections.translation: Woe is me , my son ! what art thou doing to me thy hapless sire !

sections.label: Section 1447
sections.original: ὄλωλα καὶ δὴ νερτέρων ὁρῶ πύλας .
sections.translation: I am a broken man ; yes , I see the gates that close upon the dead .

sections.label: Section 1448
sections.original: ἦ τὴν ἐμὴν ἄναγνον ἐκλιπὼν χέρα ;
sections.translation: Canst leave me thus with murder on my soul !

sections.label: Section 1449
sections.original: οὐ δῆτ ’, ἐπεί σε τοῦδ ’ ἐλευθερῶ φόνου .
sections.translation: No , no ; I set thee free from this bloodguiltiness .

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: τί φῄς ; ἀφίης αἵματός μ ’ ἐλεύθερον ;
sections.translation: What sayest thou ? dost absolve me from bloodshed ?

sections.label: Section 1451
sections.original: τὴν τοξόδαμνον Ἄρτεμιν μαρτύρομαι .
sections.translation: Artemis , the archer-queen , is my witness that I do .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθ ’, ὡς γενναῖος ἐκφαίνῃ πατρί .
sections.translation: My own dear child , how generous dost thou show thyself to thy father !

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: τοιῶνδε παίδων γνησίων εὔχου τυχεῖν .
sections.translation: Pray to have children such as me bom in lawful wedlock .

sections.label: Section 1454
sections.original: οἴμοι φρενὸς σῆς εὐσεβοῦς τε κἀγαθῆς .
sections.translation: O that holy , noble soul of thine !

sections.label: Section 1453
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε καὶ σύ , χαῖρε πολλά μοι , πάτερ .
sections.translation: Farewell , dear father ! a long farewell to thee !

sections.label: Section 1456
sections.original: μή νυν προδῷς με , τέκνον , ἀλλὰ καρτέρει .
sections.translation: O leave me not , my son ; endure awhile .

sections.label: Section 1457
sections.original: κεκαρτέρηται τἄμ ’· ὄλωλα γάρ , πάτερ .
sections.translation: ’ Tis finished , my endurance ; I die , father ; quickly cover my face with a mantle .

sections.label: Section 1459
sections.original: ὦ κλείν ’ Ἀθῆναι Παλλάδος θ ’ ὁρίσματα ,
sections.translation: O glorious Athens , realm of Pallas ,

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: οἵου στερήσεσθ ’ ἀνδρός . ὦ τλήμων ἐγώ ·
sections.translation: what a splendid hero ye have lost ! Ah me , ah me ! How oft shall I remember thy evil work , O Cypris !

sections.label: Section 1462
sections.original: κοινὸν τόδ ’ ἄχος πᾶσι πολίταις
sections.translation: On all our citizens hath come this universal sorrow , unforeseen . Now shall the copious tear gush forth ,

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: τῶν γὰρ μεγάλων ἀξιοπενθεῖς
sections.translation: for sad news about great men takes more than usual hold upon the heart .
